steps.label: Receptor binding
steps.description: Beta2 agonists bind adrenergic receptors on airway smooth muscle.

steps.label: Gs signaling
steps.description: Receptor activation stimulates Gs protein and adenylate cyclase.

steps.label: cAMP increase
steps.description: Intracellular cAMP rises and activates downstream relaxing pathways.

steps.label: MLCK inhibition
steps.description: Reduced myosin light-chain kinase activity lowers smooth muscle contraction.

steps.label: Bronchodilation
steps.description: Airway smooth muscle relaxes, improving airflow during bronchospasm.

steps.label: cAMP accumulation
steps.description: Intracellular cAMP rises excessively during overdose.

steps.label: Systemic overstimulation
steps.description: CNS excitation and cardiovascular stimulation intensify.

steps.label: Clinical toxicity
steps.description: Seizures, tachycardia, hypotension, and respiratory distress emerge.

steps.label: Countermeasure principle
steps.description: Decreasing cAMP signaling with cardioselective beta-blockade can help counter toxicity while limiting asthma risk.

steps.label: Sympathetic baseline
steps.description: Norepinephrine stimulates beta1 receptors on cardiac myocytes during sympathetic activation.

steps.label: Gs-cAMP cascade
steps.description: Beta1 signaling increases adenylate cyclase activity and cyclic AMP, amplifying calcium-mediated contraction.

steps.label: Competitive antagonism
steps.description: Metoprolol occupies beta1 receptors and reduces catecholamine-driven signaling.

steps.label: Hemodynamic reduction
steps.description: Heart rate and contractility decline, lowering cardiac output and myocardial oxygen demand.

steps.label: Clinical effect
steps.description: Blood pressure control and angina symptom improvement occur in suitable patients.
